taxonomy: Tissa media is a species of plant that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family comprises around 10,000 species of plants, many of which are woody shrubs or trees. Within the Euphorbiaceae family, Tissa media is classified as a genus of flowering plants. Further classification places Tissa media in the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The classification of Tissa media is still a subject of ongoing research and refinement in the field of botany.

preparations: Tissa media, also known as Tinspo or Thai aloe, is a plant used in traditional medicine in various forms. Herbal preparations of Tissa media are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's gel is often extracted and mixed with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments for skin care. In traditional Thai medicine, Tissa media is used to treat various health conditions, including burns, eczema, and acne. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.
